\needspace{4\baselineskip}
\section{Negroni}
\recipemeta{Rocks · Gin · Garnish: orange peel}
\recipedescription{The iconic Italian aperitif. Bold, bitter, beautiful. Equal parts gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth.}
\subsection*{Ingredients}
\begin{ingredients}
\item \textbf{gin} -- 1 oz
\item \textbf{Campari} -- 1 oz
\item \textbf{sweet vermouth} -- 1 oz
\item \textbf{large ice cube} -- 1
\item \textbf{orange peel}
\end{ingredients}
\subsection*{Instructions}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Add gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice.
\item Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled and properly diluted.
\item Strain into a rocks glass over large ice cube.
\item Express the oils from an orange peel over the drink by holding it over the glass and giving it a good twist. Rub the peel around the rim of the glass, then drop it in.
\end{enumerate}
\recipeattribution{Recipe by Count Camillo Negroni}
\vspace{3\baselineskip}
